title: notholaena tomentosa
intro: Notholaena tomentosa, also known as the woolly fern or hairy fern, is a species of fern native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Notholaena tomentosa include treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and preventing infections, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, Notholaena tomentosa is often made into teas, salves, and infusions, which are used to promote healing and overall well-being.
